title: Anthropic Files For An IPO: Rapid Reaction — With M.G. Siegler
summary: Alex Kantrowitz and M.G. Siegler discuss Anthropic's IPO filing, analyzing the timing relative to OpenAI, the competitive position of Claude, and whether the funding is strategically necessary. The conversation examines why it matters for Dario Amodei's company to go public first.
writeup: A live-news segment recorded the moment Anthropic confidentially filed a draft S-1 registration statement with the SEC, jumping ahead of OpenAI in the race to go public. The hosts trace why listing order carries serious consequences: Anthropic has reportedly dipped into profitability on rapid revenue growth, while OpenAI's MAU count has stalled at 900 million (matching Gemini) and its businesses are converging rather than diverging. The through-line is a narrative war for public investors who will draw direct comparisons between the two companies, with SpaceX's imminent IPO serving as a live stress-test of index-inclusion mechanics that could force automatic buying regardless of fundamentals.
key_points: Anthropic filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC with no advance press briefing, just two paragraphs on its blog, catching OpenAI and media off guard and seizing first-mover narrative advantage in the IPO race.

If Anthropic lists before OpenAI, the public-market damage to OpenAI is severe: Anthropic has reportedly reached at least initial profitability and overtaken OpenAI on revenue growth, making Anthropic the cleaner comp for investors choosing between the two.

OpenAI's planned counter-narrative centers on its roughly 900 million MAUs, but that number has not grown above 900 million and Google announced Gemini is also at 900 million, undercutting the differentiation.

Anthropic recently raised $65 billion and OpenAI raised $122 billion, yet Anthropic is burning significant capital leasing the SpaceX Colossus data center at a reported $15 billion per year to meet demand it cannot otherwise serve.

Proposed changes to index-inclusion rules could force mutual funds and large passive funds to automatically buy SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic shares on listing, a structural tailwind that could matter more than fundamental comparisons between the companies.

SpaceX's listing, expected within days of recording, will serve as a live test of both public appetite for large private-to-public AI-adjacent tech and the index-rebalancing effect that all three companies could benefit from.
